title: rosa villosa var. woodsiana
intro: Rosa villosa var. woodsiana, commonly known as Woods' rose or Woods' wild rose, is a variety of fragrant wild rose native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful for treating wounds, sore throats, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rosa villosa var. woodsiana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the flowers, leaves, and stems in hot water or a carrier oil.
